label: TotalNumberOfEmployees
direction: DIRECTION_UNCLEAR: Total employee count reflects operating scale, reporting boundary, outsourcing mix and automation level; it is not inherently a better/worse performance measure.

tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: FY2022-23 is consolidated while FY2023-24 is not like-for-like

sections.about: This metric measures the total number of employees disclosed at financial year-end. It falls under BRSR Section A — General Disclosures, Employees; it is not an Essential or Leadership Indicator under the principle-wise section. Direction is unclear: a larger employee base can reflect scale, legacy manpower, insourcing, or lower automation, while a smaller base can reflect productivity or outsourcing.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: SAIL has the largest reported employee base in FY2024-25 at 53,159, ahead of Tata Steel at 43,089, JSW at 14,353 and Jindal Stainless at 5,898. This is structurally consistent with SAIL’s large public-sector integrated footprint covering integrated steel plants, special steel plants, units and operating mines on a standalone basis, while Jindal Stainless is a smaller stainless steel producer and JSW’s employee base is materially lower despite large steel capacity. Tata’s FY2022-23 value of 67,784 is not comparable with FY2023-24 and FY2024-25 because the table flags it as consolidated, while Tata’s FY2024-25 BRSR also notes boundary changes and restatement of previous-period BRSR KPIs after mergers and revised reporting methodology.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 2nd on disclosed employee scale, 18.9% below SAIL. Tata moved from rank 1 in FY2022-23 to rank 2 thereafter, but this is driven mainly by boundary comparability rather than a clear operational contraction: FY2022-23 was consolidated, while later years are standardised differently. For management interpretation, this metric is best read alongside crude steel output, tonnes per employee, contractor intensity and automation across BF-BOF, mining, downstream and shared services.
sections.targets: No targets identified for this metric.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata Steel FY2022-23 is flagged as consolidated; all other company-years in the table are standalone. Tata’s FY2024-25 BRSR also notes merger-related boundary changes and restatement of BRSR KPIs, so movement from FY2022-23 to FY2023-24 should not be read as a simple workforce reduction.
